notes: v322 — COMPANY ANNOUNCEMENTS, NOW ON THE REPORT ITSELF. A tester asked: when you tap Company announcements a new tab opens — it'd be better to see the list of announcements with their dates right on the same page as the price/volume history. Done. The app already pulls REAL announcements from the official ASX feed (it uses them for the news flags and the report card's news-vs-quiet split) — it just wasn't showing you the list. Now the one-share report includes a 'Recent ASX announcements' table right under the recent-sessions history: each announcement's date and headline, with the market-sensitive ones marked ⚠. It loads that share's list automatically when you open the report (instant if it's already been checked today, a few seconds the first time for a new share). If the live feed can't be reached it says so and the official 🏛 Company announcements link is always still there for the full list. It's real ASX data — nothing is invented, and it's still a summary to verify, not advice. (ASX shares; the live feed is ASX-only.) ALSO RECENT — v321 — FIXED THE SLOWDOWN / 'PAGE UNRESPONSIVE'. A tester reported the app freezing, twice. Profiling a full 2,859-share market pinned the cause exactly: opening 📋 Signal report card re-graded EVERY signal over weeks of history from scratch on EVERY open (~0.7s on a fast desktop, several seconds on a phone), and reopening it froze the page again each time — which is precisely the 'Page Unresponsive, second time' that was seen. The auto-pilot already reused a once-a-day cache for the same maths; the report card didn't. Fixed two ways: the card now REUSES that once-a-day result, so after the first grade every reopen is instant; and on that first grade of the day it shows a 'Grading every signal…' note first, then does the work a moment later — so the tap never lands on a frozen screen. None of the grading numbers changed — it's the same honest maths, just computed once instead of every time. If you were stuck on an unresponsive page, hard-refresh (Ctrl+F5) to load this build. ALSO RECENT — v320 — 📉 DIP FINDER NOW SHOWS ON THE BUY SCREEN (not hidden behind a tab). Yesterday's v319 added the Dip Finder to the Buy dialog, but tucked it inside the Limit-buy section — so on the default 'Buy at market' tab it was invisible, and a tester couldn't find it. Fixed: the gold 📉 Dip Finder button now sits right under the Buy-at-market / Limit-buy toggle, visible on both tabs. Tap it and it measures that share's real daily dips and offers tap-to-use depths; pick one and the dialog now flips itself to a Limit order and drops the price straight into the box for you — because buying a dip IS a limit order below the close. One tap from 'what dip does this share make?' to a primed limit buy at a sensible price. ALSO RECENT — v319 — 📉 DIP FINDER, NOW INSIDE THE BUY SCREEN. Tester request: when you tap 🛒 Buy and choose a Limit buy, you no longer have to guess the price or leave the screen — a '📉 Dip Finder' button right under the price box measures THAT share's real behaviour (its actual daily dips over the last ~85 sessions) and shows its median dip plus tap-to-use price chips — its median depth, 2% and 3% — each with roughly how often a day dips that far. Tap a chip and the limit price fills itself, the how-many-shares preview updates, and nothing you typed is lost — the whole thing happens inside the Buy screen. Why it matters: one number never fits all shares — a 3-cent explorer swings 5–10% a day while a bank barely moves 1%, so a limit set by feel either never fills or fills too easily. Measuring the share you actually mean to buy replaces the guess with its own data. The full table — including the honest '+5 days after the fill' test that shows whether buying that dip actually paid — is still one tap away in ⚙ bot settings → 📉 Dip Finder. Estimates from end-of-day data, not advice. ALSO RECENT — v318 — THE COMBO GATE NOW SAYS WHAT IT WAS LOOKING FOR. A tester's diary read '… 🟢green 3 → 🧩combo 0' day after day — three shares survived every rule, then the combo gate said no, and the bare 'combo 0' looked like a fault. It wasn't: none of the three fired his chosen pair TOGETHER that day, which is exactly what a tight pair on a small pond does — pairs fire in clusters, and zero-days are proof the gate is selective, not broken. Two changes so the diary makes that obvious: the funnel stage now NAMES the requirement — '🧩🚀+🏦 0' instead of '🧩combo 0' (and '🧩📋 adaptive', '🧩🏦→🚀 chain', whichever you chose) — and a curated-pair zero now explains itself in plain words: how many shares reached the gate, why zero-days are normal, and the two levers if you want more daily action (set ⚙ source to the Smart Money list — a far bigger pond than the Top-20 — or switch the 🧩 combo gate off and let your signal ticks do the filtering). Rule of thumb from the maths: every gate you stack multiplies the odds down — a 20-share pond × quiet × RSI × day-jump × green × a same-day pair is a rare coincidence by design. Loosen until the bot trades daily, then tighten one gate at a time, watching the funnel to see what each rule really costs. ALSO RECENT — v317 — THE BOT NOW TRADES FROM DAY ONE (and the preset chips tell the truth). Follow-up to a tester's 'still getting no buys': his 🧩 combo gate was 📋 adaptive — 'follow whatever MY report card grades SOLID or PROMISING'. Honest maths has a cold-start problem: a young report card has graded NOTHING yet, so that gate closed the pool every single day. And because the 🏆 best-practice button sets that very gate, every fresh install started in a state where the bot could not buy for days. That was a design flaw, not user error — fixed properly. 📋 ADAPTIVE NOW DEGRADES GRACEFULLY: while your card has no graded pairs at all, the gate falls back to the proven starter pairs (🚀 Breakout+🏦 Inst? · 🚀+🔷 Block day · 🚀+🥇 Vol record · 🕵️ Informed?+🏦 Inst?) so the bot trades from its first day — then switches to YOUR card automatically the day any pair grades SOLID/PROMISING with positive edge. Nothing is hidden: the 📜 diary says which path ran (on buys AND on quiet days), and the armed banner shows 'your 📋 report card hasn't graded any pairs yet — the bot uses the proven starter pairs until it does'. Prefer the old wait-for-my-card behaviour? Pick 📋 strict — its label now says 'waits if none' so the trade-off is clear before you choose. PRESET CHIPS FIXED TOO: another tester ran ⚡ Hunter but the ⚖ All-rounder chip stayed highlighted — the gold was hardcoded. The highlight is now computed: whichever rule set exactly matches your saved numbers and gates lights up, hand-edited numbers light nothing, and the caption says 'your own custom mix' so you always know what you're really running. ALSO RECENT — v316 — 'NO SHARES MATCHED' — FOUND THE BUG, AND MADE THE DIARY HONEST. A tester (thanks Tony) reported the bot saying nothing met his criteria no matter the settings. Two things were going on. THE BUG: the bot's automatic once-per-day run could fire the instant fresh data appeared — BEFORE the app had finished preparing streaks, volumes and scores. At that moment the candidate pool is empty by definition, so the bot burned its daily run on an empty scan, wrote 'no shares matched', and refused to try again until tomorrow. Fixed: the automatic run now WAITS until signal preparation is finished, without using up its daily slot — it simply retries in the background every few seconds until the data is ready, then runs once, properly. (The ▶ Run-now button is unchanged — pressing it always runs immediately.) THE HONESTY FIX: when the scanner truly finds nothing, the diary now shows the FUNNEL — how many shares survived each of your rules, in order: 'pool 2,741 → smart list 38 → score≥7 11 → signals 6 → RSI 6 → day-jump 6 → 🟢green 4 → 🧩combo 0'. One glance tells you exactly which rule ate the pool — no more guessing. And when the 📋 adaptive combo gate is the culprit, the diary says why in plain words: your report card has no SOLID or PROMISING pairs yet (tiers firm up as fires build over the first days) and suggests picking a curated pair like 🚀+🏦 or switching the combo gate off while the card warms up. The status banner also now notes when signals are still preparing. A quiet day is still a quiet day — but now the bot can prove it. ALSO RECENT — THE BOT, MADE SIMPLE. Feedback said the Auto-pilot panel was getting confusing — fair call. It now opens with a plain-English STATUS BANNER that answers the only two questions that matter. WHAT DO I DO? A three-step checklist that ticks itself off as you go: ✓ 1. Tap 🏆 and press 💾 Save · ✓ 2. Switch 🤖 ON · ✓ 3. (Older notes live in the Owner’s Manual changelog.)
